name: Catalyst Screening (Volcano)
description: Catalyst screening ranks candidates by adsorbate binding energy against the Sabatier principle: too-strong binding poisons the surface and too-weak binding fails to activate reactants, so the best catalyst sits near the volcano peak with intermediate binding.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Candidate catalysts"]
    N2["Get binding energy"]
    N3{"Binding too strong?"}
    N4["Product poisons surface"]
    N5{"Binding too weak?"}
    N6["Reactant won't adsorb"]
    N7["Intermediate (Sabatier)"]
    N8["Near volcano peak"]
    N9["Test activity"]
    N10["Optimal catalyst"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|yes| N4
    N4 -->|try another| N1
    N3 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|yes| N6
    N6 -->|try another| N1
    N5 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Towards the computational design of solid catalysts
sources.authors: Nørskov, J. K.; Bligaard, T.; Rossmeisl, J.; Christensen, C. H.

sources.title: The nature of the active site in heterogeneous metal catalysis
Concepts of Modern Catalysis and Kinetics, 3rd ed. — Sabatier Analysis
